title: Agent-platform session management and routing gap wiring
summary: Implement or reconcile the remaining issue #594 agent-platform gaps: plan-phase session context injection, live session history capture, configured session cost budget enforcement, compaction triggering, and opt-in capability/model/fallback/policy routing while preserving existing orchestration compatibility defaults.
reportedGaps: Session context is not available in plan phase prompts.
Session history helpers exist but decisions, run summaries, and context snapshots are not captured during orchestration.
Session compaction logic exists but is not triggered from live state size.
Session cost budgets exist but are not enforced in the orchestration loop.
Capability router, model selection, fallback chains, and selection policies exist but are not used for task dispatch.
compatibilityRequirements: Treat #594 as stale-prone: reconcile current source first and do not duplicate wiring that already exists.
Missing, corrupt, or unrelated session context must not crash or contaminate planProcess prompts.
No-budget runs must preserve existing orchestration behavior and must not auto-pause.
Compaction must write overlay state only and never mutate original journal or task artifacts.
Default task dispatch must preserve the existing resolveTaskHarness behavior unless task metadata or execution policy explicitly opts in to policy-based routing.
Tests must use deterministic fakes and must not require provider credentials, network access, live MCP servers, or real external harnesses.
acceptanceCriteria: The run begins with a reuse audit and authoritative issue/comment read, including related #578/#580 and prior PR #657 context.
The implementation classifies each issue #594 gap as already wired, partially wired, still missing, or obsolete before changing code.
Plan-phase prompt construction includes bounded, session-scoped context when available and remains compatible when context is unavailable.
Live orchestration captures meaningful decisions, run summaries, and context snapshots using the existing session history helpers.
Configured budget enforcement is covered by tests; no-budget runs do not pause, and explicit autoPause budgets produce a clear pause/block signal.
Compaction triggering is covered by tests and writes overlay state without mutating source-of-truth journals/tasks.
Capability/model/fallback/policy routing is integrated only through explicit metadata or policy hints, with default routing compatibility tests.
Focused agent-platform tests and broad repo quality gates pass or document precise environment-only unavailable evidence.
docs/agent-layer-gaps.md is updated only when tests prove stale gap claims are now inaccurate.
